'''New Canistel''' was an inconsequential terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="G9" /> located in the [[Demetras sector/Legends|Demetras sector's]] [[New Canistel system/Legends|New Canistel system]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[senoti]] [[Tree/Legends|tree]], a [[Plant/Legends|plant]] that produced medicinal [[Sap/Legends|sap]] and could also be found on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]].<ref name="G9" />

A popular theory among [[xenoarborist]]s as to how the senoti made its way to Endor was that one or both of the [[Canistel AgriStar]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] that had been lost in the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]] had been carrying [[Seed/Legends|seeds]] of the tree and had crashed.<ref name="G9" /> Endor's conditions were not ideal for the senoti; New Canistel's alkaline soil allowed the trees to fare far better.<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' establishes that the [[senoti]] [[Tree/Legends|tree]] depicted in ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'' was among the few such trees left on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. {{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of ''Caravan of Courage'' to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after those of {{Film|IV}}, which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> very few senoti trees remained on Endor.<ref name="GATORW" />

==Behind the scenes==
New Canistel was first mentioned in the article "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]," which was published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="G9" /> on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the New Canistel [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world itself, in grid square P-7.<ref name="TEA" />
